what: Example for downloading data from an export job
title: Batch processing and export pipelines
text: example, pipeline (ID 471) needs finished download task contains two download files
code: job <- api_job_create(   endpoint = "articles/export",    params = list(projects=20, pipeline=471, id=2508),    database = "epi_playground",   payload = list(config=list(options=list(sort_signatures=0))) )   downloads <- job$downloads  api_download(downloads$url[1], filename = downloads$name[1]) api_download(downloads$url[2], filename = downloads$name[2])

title: Fetch data using the API
text: First, fetch RAM data Epigraf. data contains rows RAM tables (projects, articles, sections etc.) .

what: Map RAM data to articles and their annotations
title: Fetch data using the API
text: Second, although can directly work RAM data, suggest let rpigraf distill flat data frames . can start two perspectives: Get articles , optionally, join nested content distill_articles(). Get properties , optionally, join annotations articles distill_properties().

previous_headings: Download data > Map RAM data to articles and their annotations
title: Fetch data using the API
text: distill_articles() method pulls article data RAM data joins sections, items optionally properties used items. simple form, get list articles selected columns: join item data, provide item type columns extract items. results data frame row item (respective article columns). properties used items, add parameter defines fields extract joined property:
code: distill_articles(epi, c("signature", "name")) distill_articles(epi, c("name","signature"), item.type = "text", item.cols = "content") distill_articles(epi, c("signature", "name"), item.type = "categories", property.cols = "lemma")

previous_headings: Download data > Map RAM data to articles and their annotations
title: Fetch data using the API
text: get properties used articles (.e. properties fetched , thus, contained RAM data), use distill_properties() RAM data first parameter property type second parameter. hierarchical properties, function return property tree. get annotations, including coded segments, set annos-parameter TRUE:
code: distill_properties(epi, "categories") distill_properties(epi, "categories", annos = TRUE) distill_properties(epi, "annotations", annos = TRUE)

title: Import data using the API
text: use following simple table example:
code: ds <- tribble(   ~case, ~title,       ~genre,     ~text,   "011", "Westworld",   "Western", "In a futuristic amusement park, androids populate themed worlds like the Wild West.",   "012", "Yellowstone", "Western", "Ranch owner John Dutton battles to protect his family's massive Montana cattle ranch.",   "013", "Once Upon",   "Western", "A mysterious harmonica-playing stranger teams up with a bandit." )

title: Import data using the API
text: craft methods guide mapping process. start crafting project , step step, add articles, sections, items content. dataset remembers crafted data attribute. won’t see RAM data simply inspect data frame. intermediary columns storing last project, article section IDs visible. get RAM data, call ram_compile() last step. craft function supports two parameters: - fill used provide fixed values contained source dataset. example, project name row types usually provided filling data. - cols contains named list maps columns source data frame RAM columns respective table. example, can map text column content field items table. Let’s go step step. Project: don’t data project source data frame, fill values. project named “Movies”. type defaults “default” (optional, omit , “default” used anyways) Article data taken columns: case, title. type defaults “default”. text section named “Abstract”. omit fragment, automatically added derived article fragment. need fragment, multiple sections type. text item data taken columns: text. type fixed “text”. omit fragment, automatically added derived section fragment. need fragment, multiple items type. categories section named “Genres”. type fixed “categories”. categories property content taken genre column. crafting property, column .property added data frame. categories item content taken column.
code: ds <- craft_projects(ds, fill = c("type" = "default", "fragment" = "movies",  "name" = "Movies", "signature"="movies")) ds <- craft_articles(ds, fill = c("type" = "default"), cols=c("fragment" = "case", "signature" = "case", "name" = "title")) ds <- craft_sections(ds, fill = c("type" = "text", "name" = "Abstract")) ds <- craft_items(ds, fill = c("type" = "text"), cols = c("content" = "text")) ds <- craft_sections(ds, fill = c("type" = "categories", "name" = "Genres")) ds <- craft_properties(ds, fill = c("type" = "categories"), cols = c("fragment"="genre", "lemma" = "genre")) ds <- craft_items(ds, fill = c("type" = "categories"), cols = c("properties_id" = ".property"))

title: Import data using the API
text: Finally, compile stacked ram rows get RAM data frame. RAM data frame ready patched (.e. uploaded) Epigraf.

what: Fetch from and push data to Epigraf
title: Fetch from and push data to Epigraf
text: Rpigraf package makes data work Epigraf easier. provides functions data transfer using Epigraf APIs: Preparing data imports, e.g. social media datasets, preparing data analyses.

title: Fetch from and push data to Epigraf
Fetch from and push data to Epigraf
text: Please aware: API development responds cosy pace. Please don’t stress servers. endpoints accessing article data documented Epigraf help. get access token nonpublic data access follow instructions help. loading rpigraf package, configure connection API: access token like password, don’t show anyone make sure printed logs outputs. Note: working developer local environment, use URL https://127.0.0.1/. api_setup()-function provides third parameter enabling debug output. get “Error 401” using following methods, check permissions.

title: Fetch from and push data to Epigraf
text: warm , get article data. Data always delivered chunks, chunk called page. limit parameter tells API return 5 articles per page. maxpages parameter defines fetching stopped one page. Fetching always stopped data. data come Relation-Article-Model-format. means pieces article returned rows. Use distill-function get cozy data. example, extract properties type “categories” RAM data: extract list articles: See vignette("api_fetch", package="rpigraf") examples, e.g. get annotations.
code: articles <- api_fetch("articles", params=c(limit=5), db="epi_movies", maxpages=1) distill_properties(articles, "categories") distill_articles(articles, c("signature", "name"))

title: Fetch from and push data to Epigraf
text: can create update data api_patch(). function expects data Relational Article Model-format. following command creates one categorie “Western” IRI “properties/categories/western” database epi_movies. property given IRI path already exists, created, updated. way can change labels. property types, “categories” example, need configured Epigraf. Thereafter, can see new properties Epigraf clicking categories menu button. complex data, use craft functions map data frames RAM. See vignette("api_patch", package="rpigraf") examnples.
code: properties <- tibble(   id = c("properties/categories/western"),   lemma = c("Western") )  api_patch(properties, "epi_movies")

Patching properties prefixed ids requires type column contains property type. wide set TRUE (default), column names prefixed table names extracted. db database name. table Optional: Check data contains rows specific table. type Optional: Check data contains rows specific type. wide Convert wide format long format. TRUE, column names prefixed "properties", "items", "sections", "articles" "projects" followed dot (e.g. properties.id, properties.lemma) extracted patched additional entities.

title: Map a data frame to the Relational Article Model (RAM) — df_to_ram
text: df data frame source data. project.cols named list. Names RAM columns, values source columns. project.fill named list fixed values rows. article.cols named list. Names RAM columns, values source columns. article.fill named list fixed values rows. section.cols named list. Names RAM columns, values source columns. section.fill named list fixed values rows. property.cols named list. Names RAM columns, values source columns. property.fill named list fixed values rows. item.cols named list. Names RAM columns, values source columns. item.fill named list fixed values rows. compile Whether return compiled RAM rows RAM enhanced data frame.

title: Map a data frame to the Relational Article Model (RAM) — df_to_ram
text: compile TRUE: RAM data frame, ready patching Epigraf database. compile FALSE: RAM-enhanced data frame. RAM-enhanced data frames, RAM rows stored epi attribute. Call ram_compile() get RAM data frame RAM-enhanced data frame.

title: Map a data frame to the Relational Article Model (RAM) — df_to_ram
code: library(tibble) library(rpigraf)  df <- tribble(   ~case, ~title,     ~text,   1,     "Case 01", "Happy New Year!",   2,     "Case 02", "Happy Easter!",   3,     "Case 03", "Happy Birthday!" )  df |>  df_to_ram(    project.fill = c("fragment" = "Example"),    article.cols = c("id" = "case", "signature" = "case", "name" = "title"),    section.fill = c("fragment" = "text"),    item.cols = c("content" = "text"),    compile = TRUE  ) #> # A tibble: 8 × 8 #>   id       signature name  content sections_id articles_id projects_id `_fields` #>   <chr>    <chr>     <chr> <chr>   <chr>       <chr>       <chr>       <chr>     #> 1 project… NA        NA    NA      NA          NA          NA          id,type,… #> 2 article… 3         Case… NA      NA          NA          projects/d… projects… #> 3 article… 2         Case… NA      NA          NA          projects/d… projects… #> 4 article… 1         Case… NA      NA          NA          projects/d… projects… #> 5 section… NA        NA    NA      NA          articles/d… projects/d… articles… #> 6 items/d… NA        NA    Happy … sections/d… articles/d… projects/d… sections… #> 7 items/d… NA        NA    Happy … sections/d… articles/d… projects/d… sections… #> 8 items/d… NA        NA    Happy … sections/d… articles/d… projects/d… sections…

title: Remove all RAM rows from a tibble — ram_clear
code: library(tibble) library(rpigraf)  # Example data df <- tribble(   ~case, ~title,     ~text,   1,     "Case 01", "Happy New Year!",   2,     "Case 02", "Happy Easter!",   3,     "Case 03", "Happy Birthday!" )  # Add RAM rows df <- df |>  df_to_ram(    project.fill = c("fragment" = "Example"),    article.cols = c("id" = "case", "signature" = "case", "name" = "title"),    section.fill = c("fragment" = "text"),    item.cols = c("content" = "text")  )  ram_compile(df) #> # A tibble: 8 × 8 #>   id       signature name  content sections_id articles_id projects_id `_fields` #>   <chr>    <chr>     <chr> <chr>   <chr>       <chr>       <chr>       <chr>     #> 1 project… NA        NA    NA      NA          NA          NA          id,type,… #> 2 article… 3         Case… NA      NA          NA          projects/d… projects… #> 3 article… 2         Case… NA      NA          NA          projects/d… projects… #> 4 article… 1         Case… NA      NA          NA          projects/d… projects… #> 5 section… NA        NA    NA      NA          articles/d… projects/d… articles… #> 6 items/d… NA        NA    Happy … sections/d… articles/d… projects/d… sections… #> 7 items/d… NA        NA    Happy … sections/d… articles/d… projects/d… sections… #> 8 items/d… NA        NA    Happy … sections/d… articles/d… projects/d… sections…  # Remove RAM rows df <- ram_clear(df) ram_compile(df) #> # A tibble: 0 × 1 #> # ℹ 1 variable: value <lgl>

title: Compile a crafted table with RAM rows ready to patch into the database — ram_compile
code: library(tibble) library(rpigraf)  # Example data df <- tribble(   ~case, ~title, ~text,   1, "Case 01", "Happy New Year!",   2, "Case 02", "Happy Easter!",   3, "Case 03", "Happy Birthday!" )  # Add RAM rows df <- df |>  df_to_ram(    project.fill = c("fragment" = "Example"),    article.cols = c("id" = "case", "signature" = "case", "name" = "title"),    section.fill = c("fragment" = "text"),    item.cols = c("content" = "text")  )  ram_compile(df) #> # A tibble: 8 × 8 #>   id       signature name  content sections_id articles_id projects_id `_fields` #>   <chr>    <chr>     <chr> <chr>   <chr>       <chr>       <chr>       <chr>     #> 1 project… NA        NA    NA      NA          NA          NA          id,type,… #> 2 article… 3         Case… NA      NA          NA          projects/d… projects… #> 3 article… 2         Case… NA      NA          NA          projects/d… projects… #> 4 article… 1         Case… NA      NA          NA          projects/d… projects… #> 5 section… NA        NA    NA      NA          articles/d… projects/d… articles… #> 6 items/d… NA        NA    Happy … sections/d… articles/d… projects/d… sections… #> 7 items/d… NA        NA    Happy … sections/d… articles/d… projects/d… sections… #> 8 items/d… NA        NA    Happy … sections/d… articles/d… projects/d… sections…
